decontamination_notes: Real feature from rust-itertools/itertools PR #1104, merged 2026-05-21, after model training cutoffs. Adds strip_prefix and strip_prefix_by to the Itertools trait (the iterator analogue of str::strip_prefix) plus a public StripPrefixError struct; on mismatch the error exposes the partially-consumed iterator, the prefix remainder, and the mismatched (Option<Item>, PrefixItem) pair for recovery. Repo sliced at the PR base commit (src/lib.rs, strip_prefix absent). To run in the network-isolated sandbox, dependencies are vendored (cargo vendor -> vendor/ with .cargo/config.toml replace-source); the criterion dev-dep and [[bench]] targets were removed to keep vendoring lean, and an offline build was verified with `docker run --network none ... cargo build --offline`. LOC is inflated by the vendored crates; the itertools source change is one file. MIT/Apache-2.0 LICENSE preserved. Coverage floor: 4 fail_to_pass tests (Ok/empty/full prefix, mismatch state recovery, exhausted-iterator None, cross-type predicate via strip_prefix_by), each of which fails to even compile at base (the items do not exist), plus a strip_prefix-independent regression guard over interleave/chunks/dedup/cartesian_product.
